description: prove every JSON-object hash routes through cwp.canonical, not ad-hoc json.dumps — the P0-T04 digest-cutover acceptance criterion (F1 / P0-V03)
rules: AST same-expression lint: flags a hashlib hash call (sha*/md5/blake2*/sha3*/new) whose args contain a json.dumps(...) call
does NOT flag mere file-level co-occurrence — raw-byte hashlib (file/stdout/key hashes) and unrelated json.dumps coexist legitimately
EXCLUDE skips paths (default infra/cwp — the canonical engine itself); WHITELIST exempts known out-of-scope sites
usage: Set SCAN_ROOT (dir to scan). Optional EXCLUDE (';'-separated path substrings, default infra/cwp), WHITELIST (';'-separated relpath or relpath:line). Output: digestlint.json. Exit 0 iff no non-whitelisted json.dumps->hash site remains.
limitation: Static AST lint — it proves no json.dumps feeds a hash; it does NOT prove the canonical replacement is byte-correct (that is the vectors/crosslang perks, which replay the actual digests cross-language).
summary: prove every JSON-object hash routes through cwp.canonical, not ad-hoc json.dumps — the digest-cutover gate (P0-T04 / F1 / P0-V03)
